seoTitle: How to Log In to Your Coursera Account Quickly
metaDescription: Sign in to your Coursera account with this quick guide, including institutional login options and fixes for common login issues.
intro: Getting into your Coursera account should be quick, whether you're signing in with a standard login or through a university's institutional access. This covers the general login steps, institutional access options, and how to troubleshoot if something doesn't work.

steps.heading: Go to the Coursera login page
steps.body: Navigate to Coursera's sign-in screen to begin accessing your account.

steps.heading: Enter your login credentials
steps.body: Use your standard account details, or your university ID if you're logging in through institutional access.

steps.heading: Complete any additional verification
steps.body: Follow through any extra prompts required to finish signing in successfully.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot login issues if needed
steps.body: If you can't log in, double-check you're using the correct login method for how your account was originally set up.

faq.question: What's the difference between a standard login and institutional access?
faq.answer: A standard login uses your personal Coursera credentials, while institutional access lets you sign in through a university ID if your school partners with Coursera.

faq.question: What if I forgot my Coursera password?
faq.answer: Use the account recovery option on the login page to reset your password and regain access.

faq.question: Can I switch between a personal account and institutional access?
faq.answer: These are generally handled as separate login paths, so check which one applies to how your account was set up.

faq.question: Why is logging in reliably important for ongoing learning?
faq.answer: Consistent access lets you pick up where you left off in your courses and take advantage of Coursera's learning resources without interruption.
